concerns.text: The spec (User Story 1 Edge‑case) requires the pipeline to abort when a GEO series contains < 20 samples (see spec.md:Edge Cases). No task in the list implements this abort logic or logs the required error, leaving the requirement uncovered.

User Story 1 independent test demands that each `predicted_ppi_<species>.tsv` contain ≥ 10 000 edges (or be empty). The integration test T020 only checks that the file exists and has a header; it does not verify the edge‑count requirement, so the success criterion SC‑001 is not testable.

The evaluation implementation (T021‑T022) restricts STRING interactions to “experimental‑evidence only”. The specification (FR‑006, SC‑001) and Constitution VII only require high‑confidence interactions (combined score ≥ 700) with no evidence‑type filter. This narrowing of scope weakens the functional requirement and violates the constitution.

The integration test for US2 (T026) uses a tiny mock dataset and pre‑computed metric values. The specification’s independent test expects AUROC ≥ 0.70 and AUPRC ≥ 0.65 on the **real** dataset for each species. Testing only on mock data does not guarantee the real‑data success criteria, constituting a semantic weakening of SC‑001.

Acceptance Scenario 2 for User Story 2 requires that the baseline AUROC be ≤ 0.55. No task or test verifies this condition; the baseline generator is implemented (T023) but there is no test asserting the AUROC ceiling, leaving the acceptance criterion uncovered.
FR‑004 states the correlation threshold must never be set below 0.8. No task enforces this lower bound (e.g., validation of CLI arguments), so a user could violate the functional requirement.
Constitution VII demands AUROC > 0.70 (strictly greater). The tasks and tests only reference “≥ 0.70” (e.g., T026, T020). Without an explicit check for `>` the constitution is not satisfied.

Constitution II (Verified Accuracy) requires that all external citations be validated by the Reference‑Validator Agent. No task in the list invokes this agent or includes a verification step for citations, risking a breach of the constitution.
